ai.headline: Anthropic red-teaming 'Jupiter-V1-P' ahead of May 6 Code with Claude conference
ai.body: Anthropic has begun a fresh red-team cycle on an internal build named Jupiter-V1-P, consistent with its Responsible Scaling Policy requiring jailbreak probes and constitutional classifier stress tests before frontier deployment. The timing aligns with its Code with Claude developer conference in San Francisco on May 6, strongly suggesting an imminent model announcement. This would be a significant capability jump if released as a frontier-class model.

ai.headline: MCP supply chain attack: malicious npm package exfiltrated all outgoing emails from MCP servers
ai.body: In September 2025, a developer published a counterfeit npm package mimicking Postmark's official MCP email integration; hundreds of installs later, hidden code was found silently forwarding copies of every outgoing email to the attacker. This is documented as the first supply chain attack specifically targeting MCP server tooling, exploiting the ecosystem's rapid growth and initially lax authentication specs. MCP's auth specification has since gone through multiple revisions, but the N×M tool-integration expansion surface remains a structural risk for agentic deployments.

ai.headline: US/allies issue formal guidance on AI agent security risks: privilege abuse, identity spoofing, rogue actions
ai.body: New joint guidance from the US and allied governments explicitly calls out AI agent-specific threat vectors: privilege escalation, identity spoofing by agents impersonating users or services, unexpected autonomous actions, and corruption via malicious third-party components. This is directly relevant to any IAM or zero-trust architecture that is being extended to accommodate non-human agent identities. The guidance urges enterprises to apply least-privilege and human-approval gates for high-stakes agent actions.

ai.headline: Pentagon signs AI-to-classified-network deals with Google, Microsoft, AWS, Nvidia, OpenAI, and SpaceX
ai.body: The US Department of Defense has contracted Google, Microsoft, AWS, Nvidia, OpenAI, Reflection, and SpaceX to bring AI capabilities into classified military networks, with commitments that tools won't be used for mass surveillance or autonomous weapons. This formalises hyperscaler AI infrastructure as a component of national security architecture, accelerating classified cloud adoption across MSFT Azure Government and AWS GovCloud. For portfolio holders, this represents a durable, high-margin government revenue stream for MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, and NVDA.

ai.headline: DeepSeek-V4-Pro: 1.6T-parameter MoE, largest open-weights model, frontier-competitive at a fraction of closed-model cost
ai.body: DeepSeek-V4-Pro is a 1.6 trillion total parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with 49B active parameters and a 1M-token context window; DeepSeek-V4-Flash offers 284B total / 13B active. Both are reported as extremely cheap to serve, with V4-Pro now the largest open-weights model available. This materially compresses the cost advantage that closed frontier models held and increases competitive pressure on OpenAI and Anthropic API pricing.

ai.headline: Netflix publishes Model Lifecycle Graph architecture for democratising ML across 10+ business domains
ai.body: Netflix's 'Model Lifecycle Graph' is a production ML platform spanning personalisation, content production, studio operations, and more — far beyond the original single-domain use case. The architecture surfaces lineage, dependencies, and lifecycle state across thousands of models at scale, addressing governance and reproducibility at a level most enterprises haven't reached. Worth studying as a reference architecture for ML platform teams handling multi-domain model sprawl.

ai.headline: Perplexity publishes design principles for production Agent Skills: modular, cost-aware, multi-model tested
ai.body: Perplexity's engineering post details how it designs, refines, and maintains Agent Skills — modular capability units for its frontier agent products — with explicit guidance that each Skill has a token 'cost' requiring justification. Skills are evaluated against real queries across multiple models and continuously iterated, not treated as static software. The framework is a concrete reference for teams building agentic product layers on top of LLMs.

sec.headline: AI agent harness placement (inside vs. outside sandbox) has direct security and credential isolation implications
sec.body: Mendral's engineering post argues the LLM control loop (harness) should run outside the agent execution sandbox to keep credentials separate from untrusted code execution, enable cleaner sandbox lifecycle management, and simplify shared state in multi-user environments. Running the harness inside the sandbox risks credential leakage and cross-tenant state bleed — a concrete architectural risk as enterprises deploy multi-user agentic systems. This is a nascent but important pattern for IAM teams designing agent identity boundaries.

sec.headline: PwC + Google Cloud launch agentic AI-powered managed security service
sec.body: PwC is rolling out a managed security offering built on Google Cloud, using agentic AI to automate security operations for enterprises that have outgrown DIY SOC capabilities. The partnership is a sign that managed SIEM/SOAR is rapidly shifting toward AI-orchestrated detection and response pipelines. Relevant for teams evaluating whether to build or buy SOC automation.

fin.headline: Strait of Hormuz closure (9 weeks) now propagating into fertiliser, food supply chains — second-order inflation accelerating
fin.body: Oil at ~$120/barrel is now feeding into fertiliser shortages, which are squeezing farm input costs and tightening food production margins globally; Brent crude is up 89% since start of 2026. Early Q1 2026 earnings show energy and fertiliser sectors benefiting while food, retail, and hospitality face margin compression. For fintech fraud teams, inflationary shocks of this magnitude historically correlate with spikes in payment fraud, synthetic identity fraud, and AML-relevant cash substitution patterns.

fin.headline: Spirit Airlines collapses: 17,000 jobs lost, 1.1% domestic flight share, budget fare floor removed
fin.body: Spirit Airlines ceased operations on May 3 after failed bailout talks with the Trump administration, abruptly cancelling flights and terminating 17,000 workers. Its domestic flight share had already shrunk from 3.4% (May 2024) to 1.1% this month, limiting systemic impact, but the removal of its ultra-low fare floor gives remaining carriers pricing power. High fuel costs from the Iran war were a contributing factor, with a trade group seeking $2.5B in government relief.

swe.headline: Mercury runs 2M-line Haskell codebase in production fintech — type system encodes operational invariants at scale
swe.body: Mercury's engineering post details operating a two-million-line Haskell codebase for a fintech product with a team largely new to the language, using the type system to encode critical business invariants and make unsafe operations structurally impossible. Key patterns: treat purity as an explicit boundary, design for introspection with records of functions, use types for compliance-critical invariants rather than runtime checks. Directly relevant for IAM/fraud systems where correctness guarantees matter more than throughput.

swe.headline: OpenAI's 'harness engineering' model: agents write code, engineers build the environment and control constraints
swe.body: OpenAI's internal shift to 'harness engineering' means senior engineers focus on making codebases legible to agents (via AGENTS.md progressive disclosure, runtime state queryability) and enforcing invariants via background cleanup agents rather than code review gates. The merge philosophy prioritises fast correction over perfect prevention, accepting more noise in the short term. This pattern is emerging as the staff-engineer role in an AI-native SDLC.

swe.headline: Resilience4j annotation execution order in Spring Boot: the proxy-wrapping mental model matters for circuit breaker correctness
swe.body: In Spring Boot, Resilience4j annotations are applied as AOP proxy wrappers in reverse declaration order — meaning the bottom annotation executes first, not the top. Misunderstanding this causes circuit breakers to open prematurely and retries to fire unexpectedly, producing production behaviour that doesn't match intent. Critical to get right in any high-availability fintech service using stacked fault-tolerance primitives.

mkt.headline: Big Tech AI capex: Google Cloud reshapes AI race, sector-wide spend tops $700B; Huawei forecasts 60% AI chip revenue surge
mkt.body: Google Cloud's growth is reported as reshaping the AI competitive landscape with Big Tech aggregate AI spend now exceeding $700B. Separately, Huawei is forecasting a 60% surge in AI chip revenue — a direct signal of continued non-NVDA GPU competition in Chinese and potentially non-US markets. TSMC exited its Arm investment with a $231M share sale, a minor signal but worth noting for TSMC portfolio tracking.

mkt.headline: Microsoft reworks OpenAI partnership terms; OpenAI gains cloud flexibility as multicloud AI strategy pressure mounts
mkt.body: Microsoft and OpenAI are restructuring their partnership agreement, giving OpenAI more room to diversify infrastructure providers beyond Azure while clarifying MSFT's role as primary cloud and enterprise AI partner. This could modestly dilute MSFT's exclusive infrastructure lock on OpenAI workloads but may strengthen the commercial partnership in other dimensions. Enterprises evaluating multicloud AI strategies will watch this as a leading indicator.

mkt.headline: Anthropic nears $1.5B joint venture with Wall Street firms for enterprise AI adoption; also donated MCP to Linux Foundation
mkt.body: Anthropic is close to closing a $1.5B joint venture with Wall Street investors aimed at helping enterprises adopt AI across operations. Separately, Anthropic donated the Model Context Protocol to the newly formed Agentic AI Foundation under the Linux Foundation, co-founded with Block and OpenAI and backed by AWS, Google, Microsoft, and Cloudflare — cementing MCP as an industry standard within ~12 months of introduction. Both moves accelerate Anthropic's enterprise and ecosystem positioning ahead of a potential IPO race with OpenAI.

mkt.headline: Gartner: global IT spend heading to $6.31T in 2026; data centre investment up 50%+ driven by AI infrastructure
mkt.body: Gartner projects 2026 global IT spend at $6.31 trillion, with AI infrastructure driving a more than 50% increase in data centre investment. This validates continued hyperscaler capex expansion and supports the investment thesis for AMZN, MSFT, GOOGL, and NVDA. The figures also reinforce TSMC's structural position as the leading-edge foundry for AI accelerators.

mkt.headline: Replit nearing $1B ARR with 300% NRR and positive gross margins — contrasts sharply with Cursor's negative-margin position
mkt.body: Replit's CEO reports the company is approaching a billion-dollar revenue run rate with 300% net revenue retention and positive gross margins, differentiating it from Cursor which is reportedly running negative margins despite high growth. Replit targets non-technical users with a secure end-to-end platform, while acknowledging ongoing acquisition discussions. This positions Replit as a credible M&A target for a hyperscaler seeking an integrated agentic dev platform.

deepDives.title: MCP Tool-Use Security: Supply Chain Attack Anatomy and Protocol Evolution
deepDives.why: The first documented MCP supply-chain attack combined with new US/allied AI agent security guidance makes this the highest-priority reading for anyone designing agentic systems with external tool integrations or non-human identity boundaries.

deepDives.title: Mercury's 2M-Line Haskell Fintech Codebase: Type-Safe Invariants at Scale
deepDives.why: Rare production case study from a fintech company using the type system as a correctness enforcement mechanism for compliance-critical code — directly applicable to IAM, fraud, and risk system design.

deepDives.title: Perplexity Agent Skills Design: Modular, Cost-Aware Agentic Architecture in Production
deepDives.why: Concrete engineering reference for building maintainable agentic capability layers with explicit token-cost accounting and multi-model evaluation — a pattern needed by any team moving from prototype to production agents.
